message.content:
=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
A source may enter the corpus ONLY bound to real bytes through provenance.py: manifestation_id +
content_hash, and for task 72's JOURNAL-ONLY policy it must be journal-attributable (a working paper is a
DISCOVERY LEAD, not journal evidence — peer review changes numbers; Acemoglu's robot effect was 0.37pp in
the NBER WP and 0.2pp in the JPE). Every fetch goes through acquisition.Acquirer, which emits observations,
never statuses. A 429 is THROTTLED -> BACKEND_FAILED, NEVER "no evidence exists".

=== THE TWO GUARDRAILS — WE HAVE HURT OURSELVES ON BOTH BEFORE ===
1. MAX THE SEARCH, NOT THE COUNT. Sol: the objective is NOT "100 papers" or "202 numbers" — it is ENOUGH
   INDEPENDENT, INTERPRETABLE EVIDENCE to close the question's cells and create high-value CONTRASTS.
   bodhi scores 0.5441 with ONE venue mention. So: cast the widest possible net, but ADMIT ONLY genuinely
   on-topic journal articles. A keyword net once dragged in ResNet, SMOTE, skin-cancer classification and
   the European Heart Journal ('work' is a substring of 'network'). Semantic on-topic gate, WORD BOUNDARIES.
2. GENERALITY. Run the CONTRACT-DRIVEN pipeline (research_contract -> source_router), NOT a hardcoded
   task-72 regex. We are running it ON task 72; we are not baking task 72 into the code.

=== DO NOT COLLIDE ===
Two other wheels are live (composer wiring, generality). Write ONLY to outputs/acquisition_campaign/.
Do NOT touch cellcog_composer.py, outputs/journal_corpus_content.json, or outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json.
acquisition.py's ledger is append-only union — safe to share.


=== WHERE WE ARE ===
10 admissible journal works (the honest post-quarantine corpus). cellcog #1 has ~98 sources; bodhi #2 ~33.
This is a FLOOR, not SOTA. The seed works, with DOIs to expand from:
  Acemoglu 2019 JEP | Goos 2014 AER | Damioli 2021 Eurasian Bus Rev | Schwabe 2020 PLOS ONE |
  Tolan 2021 J.AI Research | Cao 2021 Technovation | Braganza 2021 J.Bus.Research | Raj 2019 J.Org.Design |
  Ayling 2022 AI&Ethics | Chalmers 2021 Entrep.Theory&Practice

EMPTY COVERAGE CELLS (the outline promises sections we cannot support): healthcare, manufacturing, retail,
finance, education, agriculture, transport. And our corpus ENDS IN 2023 — the entire generative-AI turn
(2023-2025) is missing, and the judge rewards current evidence.

TOOLS ON DISK (read them, use them, do not rebuild): source_router.py (+ config/source_routes.yaml),
recency.py, gap_search.py, insight_value.py, weighting.py, journal_corpus_build.py (has citation-graph
expansion that once took us 22->70 papers), deep_fetch.py, wp_fetch.py, acquisition.py, provenance.py,
research_contract.py.


The fetch phase reported:
The run is proceeding through Tier 0. I'll let the monitor stream milestones and report when it completes. Waiting for progress events.

YOUR TASK — THE HONEST ADMISSION CENSUS. For everything fetched into outputs/acquisition_campaign/:
 1. Build/extend the provenance graph over the new manifestations. Derive each one's artifact_kind and
    completeness FROM THE BYTES (a judicial opinion is complete at 105 words; a cookie banner is not a
    paper at 100,000). A landing page is NOT a journal article — Frey & Osborne's ORA page fooled us once.
 2. For each, decide admissibility under the JOURNAL-ONLY policy: is it a bound, complete, journal-
    attributable manifestation, genuinely on-topic? Working papers/preprints -> DISCOVERY LEAD (not
    task-72 admissible). Landing pages / wrong-work / abstract-only -> QUARANTINE.
 3. Produce the census:
      - NEW admissible journal works added (the number that matters) — 10 -> ?
      - which empty coverage cells now have >=2 independent works (healthcare, manufacturing, retail,
        finance, education, agriculture, transport)
      - how many 2023-2025 frontier works admitted
      - genuine CONTRASTS gained (papers that disagree with the seed set)
      - honestly unreachable: paywalled-no-OA count, and throttled-retriable count (NOT the same thing)
 4. Do NOT merge into the shipping corpus yet — that is a scored decision for the A6 arm. Just report what
    a merge WOULD add, with every source's binding.

Be brutal and honest. If max-effort search still only yields, say, 25 admissible works, SAY SO — that tells
us the ceiling is access, not effort, and that is a real finding. If it yields 60+, say that too. Return the
full census.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""BULK FETCH — full text for the DEDUPED UNION of the four discovery scouts.
3	
4	THE ONE DOOR. Every network request goes through acquisition.Acquirer; every set of
5	recovered bytes is content-addressed and profiled by acquisition.record_manifestation
6	(== deep_fetch.fetch_text). This file writes NO status and has no API that could: the
7	per-unit outcome is DERIVED from the ledger by event_ledger's reducers, so a THROTTLE
8	(429) lands as BACKEND_FAILED -> retriable, and NEVER as "no free copy exists".
9	
10	ISOLATION (DO NOT COLLIDE): reads the scouts' outputs; writes ONLY under
11	    outputs/acquisition_campaign/ (fetch_ledger.jsonl, blobs/, fetch_*.json).
12	It never touches journal_corpus_content.json, evidence_cards_bound.json, or the
13	composer. The ledger + blob store here are private to this campaign.
14	
15	POLITENESS (we IP-throttled ourselves overnight): >=1.3s spacing per host (Acquirer),
16	exponential backoff on 429 (Acquirer), plus a per-host circuit breaker that stops
17	hammering a host after repeated throttles — recorded as a budget/throttle skip, which
18	is NOT an evidence gap.
19	
20	PRIORITY (max the search, not the count): the outline's EMPTY coverage cells

23	within a tier, a transparent quality blend orders the rest. weighting.py's core rule is
24	honoured: field-normalized influence is UNKNOWN offline (never 0), and raw citations are
25	a weak, capped tie-break only — never the ranking key. Everything not reached in the
26	budget is LOGGED as DEFERRED with a reason. No silent truncation.

29	    python3 bulk_fetch.py fetch --max-units N --wall-clock-mins M [--sample K] [--tiers 0,1,2]

125	def _add(units: dict, key: str, **fields):
126	    u = units.setdefault(key, {
127	        'unit_key': key, 'doi': '', 'title': '', 'venue': '', 'year': None,
128	        'authors': [], 'type': '', 'openalex_id': '', 'cells': set(), 'scouts': set(),
129	        'roles': set(), 'genai_specific': False, 'cited_by': 0, 'n_seed_links': 0,
130	        'is_oa_hint': False, 'admissible': True, 'why': [],
131	    })
132	    for k, v in fields.items():
133	        if v in (None, '', [], set()):
134	            continue
135	        if k in ('cells', 'scouts', 'roles'):
136	            u[k] |= set(v if isinstance(v, (set, list, tuple)) else [v])
137	        elif k == 'cited_by':
138	            u['cited_by'] = max(u['cited_by'], int(v or 0))
139	        elif k == 'n_seed_links':
140	            u['n_seed_links'] = max(u['n_seed_links'], int(v or 0))
141	        elif k in ('genai_specific', 'is_oa_hint'):
142	            u[k] = u[k] or bool(v)
143	        elif k == 'admissible':
144	            u[k] = u[k] and bool(v)                 # any scout flagging inadmissible wins
145	        elif k == 'why':
146	            u['why'].append(str(v)[:220])
147	        elif k == 'authors':
148	            if len(v) > len(u['authors']):
149	                u['authors'] = list(v)
150	        elif not u.get(k):
151	            u[k] = v
152	    return u

155	def build_union() -> dict:
156	    units: dict = {}
157	
158	    # -- modality 1: citation-graph expansion --------------------------------------------
159	    for r in json.loads(M1.read_text()):
160	        k = unit_key(r.get('doi'), r.get('title'))

179	        _add(units, k, doi=norm_doi(r.get('doi')), title=r.get('title'), venue=r.get('venue'),
180	             year=r.get('year'), type='journal-article', authors=r.get('authors') or [],
181	             cells={c for c in cells if c}, scouts='m2_empty_cells',

217	def harvest_offline_oa(units: dict) -> dict:
218	    want_oaid = {u['openalex_id'] for u in units.values() if u['openalex_id']}
219	    want_doi = {norm_doi(u['doi']) for u in units.values() if u['doi']}
220	    oa: dict = {}                                    # unit lookup-id -> {'pdf':[...], 'landing':[...]}
221	    if not POOL_META.exists():
222	        return oa
223	    n = 0
224	    with open(POOL_META) as fh:
225	        for line in fh:
226	            n += 1
227	            try:
228	                d = json.loads(line)
229	            except Exception:
230	                continue
231	            oaid = (d.get('id') or '').replace('https://openalex.org/', '')
232	            doi = norm_doi(d.get('doi'))
233	            if oaid not in want_oaid and doi not in want_doi:
234	                continue
235	            pdfs, landings = [], []
236	            best = d.get('best_oa_location') or {}
237	            if best.get('pdf_url'):
238	                pdfs.append(best['pdf_url'])
239	            if best.get('landing_page_url'):
240	                landings.append(best['landing_page_url'])
241	            for loc in (d.get('locations') or []):
242	                if not loc.get('is_oa'):
243	                    continue
244	                if loc.get('pdf_url'):
245	                    pdfs.append(loc['pdf_url'])
246	                elif loc.get('landing_page_url'):
247	                    landings.append(loc['landing_page_url'])
248	            rec = {'pdf': list(dict.fromkeys(pdfs)), 'landing': list(dict.fromkeys(landings))}
249	            if oaid:

251	            if doi:

266	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

268	def recency_score(year) -> float:
269	    try:
270	        y = int(year)
271	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
272	        return 0.40                                  # UNKNOWN year -> neutral, NOT 0
273	    if y >= 2024:
274	        return 1.0
275	    if y == 2023:
276	        return 0.9
277	    if y >= 2020:
278	        return 0.6
279	    if y >= 2015:
280	        return 0.4
281	    return 0.25
282	
283	
284	def priority_of(u: dict) -> dict:
285	    has_empty = bool(u['cells'] & EMPTY_CELLS)
286	    # Confidence of the empty-cell signal: m2 passed a SEMANTIC industry-vocabulary gate;
287	    # m1's coverage_cells is a looser keyword co-occurrence net (it tagged Autor-Levy-Murnane
288	    # 2003 'education'). So a confirmed empty cell requires the m2 scout (or a canon/genai hit).
289	    confirmed_cell = ('m2_empty_cells' in u['scouts']) and has_empty
290	    is_canon = bool(u['roles'])                                  # m4, hand-verified
291	    genai = u['genai_specific']                                  # m3, generative-AI turn
292	    m1_industry = ('m1_citation_graph' in u['scouts']) and has_empty
293	    yr_recent = bool(u['year']) and int(u['year']) >= 2023
294	
295	    # TIER — the outline's BLANK cells + the gen-AI turn + the canon contrasts come first.
296	    if confirmed_cell or is_canon or genai:
297	        tier = 0
298	    elif yr_recent or m1_industry:
299	        tier = 1
300	    else:
301	        tier = 2
302	
303	    cell_priority = (1.0 if confirmed_cell else 0.85 if is_canon
304	                     else 0.6 if m1_industry else 0.4)
305	    recency = recency_score(u['year'])
306	    graph_centrality = min(u['n_seed_links'], 5) / 5.0 if u['n_seed_links'] else 0.5
307	    fame = min(1.0, math.log10((u['cited_by'] or 0) + 1) / 4.0)   # weak, capped, log-scaled
308	
309	    score = (0.40 * cell_priority + 0.25 * recency + 0.15 * graph_centrality
310	             + 0.10 * (1.0 if genai else 0.0) + 0.10 * fame)
311	    return {'tier': tier, 'score': round(score, 4),
312	            'cell_priority': cell_priority, 'recency': recency,
313	            'graph_centrality': round(graph_centrality, 3), 'fame_tiebreak': round(fame, 3),
314	            'has_empty_cell': has_empty, 'confirmed_empty_cell': confirmed_cell,
315	            'is_canon': is_canon, 'genai': genai}
316	
317	
318	def rank(units: dict) -> list[dict]:
319	    rows = []
320	    for u in units.values():
321	        p = priority_of(u)
322	        r = dict(u)
323	        r['cells'] = sorted(u['cells'])
324	        r['scouts'] = sorted(u['scouts'])
325	        r['roles'] = sorted(u['roles'])
326	        r['priority'] = p
327	        rows.append(r)
328	    rows.sort(key=lambda r: (r['priority']['tier'], -r['priority']['score'],
329	                             -(r['cited_by'] or 0)))
330	    return rows
331

333	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
334	# 4. LOCATION DISCOVERY — try EVERY OA route. Each backend goes through the Acquirer, so
335	#    a 429 is on the ledger as THROTTLED, never as "no copy".
336	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
337	
338	class Hosts:
339	    """Per-host circuit breaker: stop calling a host after THROTTLE_GIVEUP consecutive
340	    429s. Skipping a throttled host is a BUDGET decision (recorded), not an absence."""
341	    def __init__(self):
342	        self.consec: dict = {}
343	        self.cool: set = set()
344	
345	    def note(self, host: str, outcome: str):
346	        if outcome == BACKEND_FAILED:
347	            self.consec[host] = self.consec.get(host, 0) + 1
348	            if self.consec[host] >= THROTTLE_GIVEUP:
349	                self.cool.add(host)
350	        elif outcome in (RESPONDED, NOT_INDEXED):
351	            self.consec[host] = 0
352	
353	    def hot(self, host: str) -> bool:
354	        return host not in self.cool
355	
356	
357	def _europepmc_urls(acq, unit, doi, hosts) -> list[str]:
358	    host = 'europepmc'
359	    if not hosts.hot(host):
360	        return []
361	    q = f'DOI:%22{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}%22'
362	    url = (f'https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search?query={q}'
363	           f'&format=json&resultType=core&pageSize=1')
364	    r, data = acq.get_json(unit, host, url, tries=2)
365	    hosts.note(host, derive_backend_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), host))
366	    urls = []
367	    try:
368	        res = (data.get('resultList') or {}).get('result') or []
369	        if res:
370	            rec = res[0]
371	            pmcid = rec.get('pmcid')
372	            if pmcid:
373	                fx = (f'https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/'
374	                      f'{pmcid}/fullTextXML')
375	                urls.append(fx)
376	                acq.candidate(unit, host, fx, host_type='pmc_fulltext_xml')
377	            for fu in ((rec.get('fullTextUrlList') or {}).get('fullTextUrl') or []):
378	                if fu.get('availability', '').lower().startswith('open') and fu.get('url'):
379	                    urls.append(fu['url'])
380	                    acq.candidate(unit, host, fu['url'],
381	                                  host_type=str(fu.get('documentStyle') or ''))
382	    except Exception:
383	        pass
384	    return list(dict.fromkeys(urls))

387	def _doaj_urls(acq, unit, doi, hosts) -> list[str]:
388	    host = 'doaj'
389	    if not hosts.hot(host):
390	        return []
391	    url = f'https://doaj.org/api/search/articles/doi:{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}'
392	    r, data = acq.get_json(unit, host, url, tries=2)
393	    hosts.note(host, derive_backend_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), host))
394	    urls = []
395	    try:
396	        for res in (data.get('results') or [])[:1]:
397	            for lk in ((res.get('bibjson') or {}).get('link') or []):
398	                if lk.get('url') and lk.get('type', '') in ('fulltext', ''):
399	                    urls.append(lk['url'])
400	                    acq.candidate(unit, host, lk['url'], host_type=str(lk.get('type') or ''))
401	    except Exception:
402	        pass
403	    return list(dict.fromkeys(urls))
404	
405	
406	def _openalex_urls(acq, unit, doi, hosts) -> list[str]:
407	    host = 'openalex'
408	    if not hosts.hot(host):
409	        return []
410	    url = f'https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:{urllib.parse.quote(doi)}?mailto={MAILTO}'
411	    r, data = acq.get_json(unit, host, url, tries=2)
412	    hosts.note(host, derive_backend_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), host))
413	    urls = []
414	    try:
415	        best = data.get('best_oa_location') or {}
416	        if best.get('pdf_url'):
417	            urls.append(best['pdf_url'])
418	        for loc in (data.get('locations') or []):
419	            if loc.get('is_oa') and loc.get('pdf_url'):
420	                urls.append(loc['pdf_url'])
421	        for u2 in urls:
422	            acq.candidate(unit, host, u2, host_type='openalex_oa')
423	    except Exception:
424	        pass
425	    return list(dict.fromkeys(urls))
426	
427	
428	def discover_locations(acq, unit, u, offline_oa, hosts, deep: bool) -> list[str]:
429	    """The cascade. `deep` adds Europe PMC / DOAJ / live OpenAlex for the priority tiers."""
430	    urls: list[str] = []
431	    urls += offline_urls_for(u, offline_oa)          # 1. offline OpenAlex (free)
432	    doi = norm_doi(u['doi'])
433	    if doi:
434	        # 2. Unpaywall — canonical OA aggregator; emits its own candidate/backend events.
435	        if hosts.hot('unpaywall'):
436	            try:
437	                urls += deep_fetch.all_oa_locations(acq, unit, doi)
438	            except Exception:
439	                pass
440	            hosts.note('unpaywall', derive_backend_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), 'unpaywall'))
441	        if deep:
442	            urls += _europepmc_urls(acq, unit, doi, hosts)    # 3. Europe PMC / PMC full text
443	            if not u['openalex_id']:
444	                urls += _openalex_urls(acq, unit, doi, hosts)  # 4. live OpenAlex (if not offline)
445	            urls += _doaj_urls(acq, unit, doi, hosts)          # 5. DOAJ
446	    # de-dup, keep order (offline pdfs first)
447	    seen, out = set(), []
448	    for x in urls:
449	        if x and x not in seen:
450	            seen.add(x)
451	            out.append(x)
452	    return out
453	
454	
455	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
456	# 5. PER-UNIT HONEST OUTCOME — a reducer over the ledger. THROTTLED stays distinct from
457	#    absence; a content result (fulltext/abstract/landing/wrong-work) beats a transport
458	#    failure for the primary label, but a throttle is flagged retriable regardless.
459	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
460	
461	def classify_unit(ledger, unit: str) -> dict:
462	    evs = ledger.events(unit)
463	    if not evs:
464	        return {'outcome': 'NOT_ATTEMPTED'}
465	    cls, info = derive_content_profile(evs)
466	    kind = info.get('artifact_kind', '')
467	    binding, _b = derive_semantic_binding(evs)
468	    route = derive_route_status(evs)
469	    outs = set(route.outcomes.values())
470	    throttled = BACKEND_FAILED in outs
471	    blocked = ACCESS_BLOCKED in outs
472	
473	    if cls == C_FULLTEXT:
474	        outcome = 'FETCHED'
475	    elif binding == DIFFERENT_WORK or kind == 'wrong_work':
476	        outcome = 'WRONG_WORK'
477	    elif cls == C_ABSTRACT:
478	        outcome = 'ABSTRACT_ONLY'
479	    elif kind == 'landing_page':
480	        outcome = 'LANDING_PAGE'
481	    elif cls == C_UNREADABLE or kind == 'extraction_failure':
482	        outcome = 'UNREADABLE_ENCODING'
483	    else:
484	        # We hold nothing usable. The transport tells us WHY — and a throttle is retriable.
485	        if throttled:
486	            outcome = 'THROTTLED'
487	        elif blocked:
488	            outcome = 'ACCESS_DENIED'
489	        else:
490	            outcome = 'NOT_FOUND'
491	    return {
492	        'outcome': outcome,
493	        'content_class': cls,
494	        'artifact_kind': kind,
495	        'binding': binding,
496	        'route_state': route.state,
497	        'fulltext_retriable': bool(throttled and outcome != 'FETCHED'),
498	        'access_blocked_seen': blocked,
499	        'readable_words': info.get('readable_word_count') or info.get('word_count') or 0,
500	        'n_docs_held': info.get('n_documents_held', 1) if cls != C_CITATION else 0,
501	    }
502	
503	
504	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
505	# 6. SUBCOMMANDS
506	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
507	
508	def cmd_build(args) -> int:
509	    units = build_union()
510	    n_doi = sum(1 for u in units.values() if u['doi'])
511	    log(f'UNION: {len(units):,} unique units ({n_doi:,} with DOI, '
512	        f'{len(units) - n_doi} title-only).')
513	    offline = harvest_offline_oa(units)
514	    ranked = rank(units)
515	    for r in ranked:
516	        r['offline_oa_urls'] = offline_urls_for(r, offline)
517	
518	    # coverage census
519	    by_tier = {0: 0, 1: 0, 2: 0}
520	    by_cell = {c: 0 for c in EMPTY_CELLS}
521	    by_scout = {}
522	    n_offline = 0
523	    for r in ranked:
524	        by_tier[r['priority']['tier']] += 1
525	        for c in r['cells']:
526	            if c in by_cell:
527	                by_cell[c] += 1
528	        for s in r['scouts']:
529	            by_scout[s] = by_scout.get(s, 0) + 1
530	        if r['offline_oa_urls']:
531	            n_offline += 1
532	
533	    UNION_OUT.write_text(json.dumps({
534	        'generated': time.strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S'),
535	        'union_size': len(units),
536	        'with_doi': n_doi, 'title_only': len(units) - n_doi,
537	        'by_tier': by_tier, 'empty_cell_coverage': by_cell, 'by_scout': by_scout,
538	        'units_with_offline_oa_url': n_offline,
539	        'dedup_rule': 'normalized DOI, then normalized title where DOI is missing',
540	        'tiers': {'0': 'empty coverage cell OR canon/contrast OR generative-AI-specific',
541	                  '1': '2023-2025 frontier (remainder)',
542	                  '2': 'citation-graph expansion (general/labor cells)'},
543	    }, indent=1))
544	    RANKED_OUT.write_text(json.dumps(ranked, indent=1))
545	    log(f'tiers: T0(coverage-critical)={by_tier[0]:,}  T1(frontier)={by_tier[1]:,}  '
546	        f'T2(graph)={by_tier[2]:,}')
547	    log(f'empty-cell units: {by_cell}')
548	    log(f'units with an OFFLINE OA url (no network needed to find a copy): {n_offline:,}')
549	    log(f'wrote {UNION_OUT.name} and {RANKED_OUT.name}')
550	    return 0
551	
552	
553	def cmd_fetch(args) -> int:
554	    if not RANKED_OUT.exists():
555	        cmd_build(args)
556	    ranked = json.loads(RANKED_OUT.read_text())
557	    offline = {}                                     # already inlined into ranked rows
558	
559	    tiers = {int(t) for t in str(args.tiers).split(',')} if args.tiers else {0, 1, 2}
560	    targets = [r for r in ranked if r['priority']['tier'] in tiers and r['admissible']]
561	    if args.sample:
562	        targets = targets[:args.sample]
563	
564	    ledger = open_ledger(LEDGER)
565	    blobs = BlobStore(BLOBS)
566	    acq = Acquirer('bulk_fetch', ledger=ledger, blobs=blobs)
567	    hosts = Hosts()
568	
569	    log(f'=== FETCH: {len(targets):,} target units (tiers {sorted(tiers)}), '
570	        f'budget: max_units={args.max_units}, wall_clock={args.wall_clock_mins}min ===')
571	    log(f'ledger={LEDGER}  ({len(ledger)} events already on record)  blobs={BLOBS}')
572	
573	    t0 = time.time()
574	    deadline = t0 + args.wall_clock_mins * 60
575	    done = 0
576	    hist: dict = {}
577	    processed_keys = set()
578	
579	    for i, r in enumerate(targets):
580	        if done >= args.max_units:
581	            log(f'-- MAX_UNITS budget ({args.max_units}) reached at target #{i}. Stopping. --')
582	            break
583	        if time.time() >= deadline:
584	            log(f'-- WALL_CLOCK budget ({args.wall_clock_mins}min) reached at target #{i}. '
585	                f'Stopping. --')
586	            break
587	
588	        unit = r['unit_key']
589	        # RESUME / idempotent: if the ledger already holds a COMPLETE document for this unit
590	        # (this run or a prior one), don't spend a network request re-fetching it.
591	        if classify_unit(ledger, unit).get('outcome') == 'FETCHED':
592	            processed_keys.add(unit)
593	            done += 1
594	            continue
595	        doi = norm_doi(r['doi'])
596	        deep = r['priority']['tier'] in (0, 1)       # richer route for the priority tiers
597	
598	        # 1. ROUTE_PLANNED — named before we walk it, so route_complete can mean something.
599	        planned = ['unpaywall', 's2/abstract']
600	        if deep:
601	            planned += ['europepmc', 'doaj']
602	            if not r['openalex_id']:
603	                planned.append('openalex')
604	        acq.plan_route(unit, planned, requested_title=r['title'] or '', doi=doi,
605	                       requested_venue=r['venue'] or '', requested_year=r['year'],
606	                       source_type=r['type'] or 'journal-article')
607	
608	        row = {'title': r['title'], 'authors': r['authors'], 'doi': doi,
609	               'venue': r['venue'], 'year': r['year'], 'type': r['type'] or 'journal-article'}
610	
611	        # 2. discover OA locations across every route
612	        locs = discover_locations(acq, unit, r, offline, hosts, deep)
613	
614	        # 3. fetch the bytes (content hosts are diverse -> the per-host spacing rarely bites)
615	        for url in locs[:MAX_LOCATIONS]:
616	            ch = content_host(url)
617	            if not hosts.hot(ch):
618	                continue
619	            try:
620	                deep_fetch.fetch_text(acq, unit, url, row)
621	            except Exception as e:
622	                log(f'   fetch error {unit}: {type(e).__name__}')
623	            hosts.note(ch, derive_backend_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), ch))
624	            # stop early if we already hold a complete document
625	            cls, _ = derive_content_profile(acq.ledger.events(unit))
626	            if cls == C_FULLTEXT:
627	                break
628	        if len(locs) > MAX_LOCATIONS:
629	            acq.budget_stopped(unit, n_locations_known=len(locs), n_locations_tried=MAX_LOCATIONS,
630	                               reason_text=f'tried {MAX_LOCATIONS} of {len(locs)} known OA locations')
631	
632	        # 4. an abstract floor from Semantic Scholar (skipped if S2 is cooling)
633	        cls_now, _ = derive_content_profile(acq.ledger.events(unit))
634	        if cls_now != C_FULLTEXT and doi and hosts.hot('content:api.semanticscholar.org') \
635	                and hosts.hot('s2'):
636	            try:
637	                pdf = deep_fetch.semantic_scholar_pdf(acq, unit, doi)
638	                hosts.note('s2', derive_backend_outcome(acq.ledger.events(unit), 's2/oa-pdf'))
639	                if pdf and hosts.hot(content_host(pdf)):
640	                    deep_fetch.fetch_text(acq, unit, pdf, row)
641	            except Exception:
642	                pass
643	
644	        outc = classify_unit(ledger, unit)
645	        hist[outc['outcome']] = hist.get(outc['outcome'], 0) + 1
646	        processed_keys.add(unit)
647	        done += 1
648	
649	        tag = f"T{r['priority']['tier']}"
650	        cell = ','.join(sorted(set(r['cells']) & EMPTY_CELLS)) or '-'
651	        if done % 25 == 0:
652	            el = time.time() - t0
653	            fetched_so_far = sum(v for k, v in hist.items() if k == 'FETCHED')
654	            log(f"  [{done:>4}/{len(targets)}] {tag} cell={cell:<12} last={outc['outcome']:<14} "
655	                f"fetched~{fetched_so_far}  cool_hosts={sorted(hosts.cool)}  {el/60:.1f}min")
656	
657	    # -- FINAL: derive outcomes over the WHOLE ledger, and log what we deferred ----------
658	    write_outcomes(ledger, ranked, processed_keys, hosts,
659	                   budget={'max_units': args.max_units, 'wall_clock_mins': args.wall_clock_mins,
660	                           'tiers': sorted(tiers), 'elapsed_min': round((time.time() - t0) / 60, 1)})
661	    return 0
662	
663	
664	def write_outcomes(ledger, ranked, processed_keys, hosts, budget) -> None:
665	    hist: dict = {}
666	    retriable = 0
667	    per_unit = []
668	    fetched_units = []
669	    for r in ranked:
670	        unit = r['unit_key']
671	        if unit not in processed_keys:
672	            continue
673	        c = classify_unit(ledger, unit)
674	        hist[c['outcome']] = hist.get(c['outcome'], 0) + 1
675	        if c.get('fulltext_retriable'):
676	            retriable += 1
677	        rec = {'unit_key': unit, 'doi': r['doi'], 'title': r['title'],
678	               'tier': r['priority']['tier'], 'cells': r['cells'], 'scouts': r['scouts'],
679	               **c}
680	        per_unit.append(rec)
681	        if c['outcome'] == 'FETCHED':
682	            fetched_units.append(rec)
683	
684	    deferred = [{'unit_key': r['unit_key'], 'doi': r['doi'], 'title': r['title'],
685	                 'tier': r['priority']['tier'], 'cells': r['cells'],
686	                 'priority_score': r['priority']['score'],
687	                 'reason': 'below the budget cutoff in priority order — NOT an absence; retriable'}
688	                for r in ranked if r['unit_key'] not in processed_keys and r['admissible']]
689	    inadmissible = [{'unit_key': r['unit_key'], 'doi': r['doi'], 'title': r['title'],
690	                     'reason': 'flagged non-journal / inadmissible under task-72 journal-only policy'}
691	                    for r in ranked if not r['admissible']]
692	
693	    # empty-cell yield: how many of the outline's blank cells did we actually close?
694	    cell_fetched = {c: 0 for c in EMPTY_CELLS}
695	    cell_total = {c: 0 for c in EMPTY_CELLS}
696	    for rec in per_unit:
697	        for c in rec['cells']:
698	            if c in cell_total:
699	                cell_total[c] += 1
700	                if rec['outcome'] == 'FETCHED':
701	                    cell_fetched[c] += 1
702	
703	    OUTCOMES_OUT.write_text(json.dumps({
704	        'generated': time.strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S'),
705	        'union_size': len(ranked),
706	        'attempted': len(processed_keys),
707	        'deferred': len(deferred),
708	        'inadmissible_excluded': len(inadmissible),
709	        'budget': budget,
710	        'outcome_histogram': dict(sorted(hist.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])),
711	        'fetched_count': hist.get('FETCHED', 0),
712	        'throttled_retriable_note': (
713	            f'{hist.get("THROTTLED", 0)} units are THROTTLED (429/503) and '
714	            f'{retriable} more hold a partial doc whose full text is retriable — '
715	            f'a THROTTLED is NOT an absence; re-run to recover.'),
716	        'empty_cell_fetched': cell_fetched,
717	        'empty_cell_attempted': cell_total,
718	        'cooling_hosts': sorted(hosts.cool),
719	        'per_unit_outcomes': per_unit,
720	        'fetched_units': fetched_units,
721	        'deferred_units': deferred,
722	        'inadmissible_units': inadmissible,
723	    }, indent=1))
724	    log('=== OUTCOME HISTOGRAM (honest, derived from the ledger) ===')
725	    for k, v in sorted(hist.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
726	        log(f'   {k:<20} {v:>5}')
727	    log(f'attempted={len(processed_keys)}  fetched={hist.get("FETCHED", 0)}  '
728	        f'deferred={len(deferred)}  throttled(retriable)={hist.get("THROTTLED", 0)}+{retriable}')
729	    log(f'empty-cell fulltext yield: {cell_fetched}')
730	    log(f'wrote {OUTCOMES_OUT.name}')
731	
732	
733	def cmd_report(args) -> int:
734	    ledger = open_ledger(LEDGER)
735	    ranked = json.loads(RANKED_OUT.read_text())
736	    processed = set(ledger.units())
737	    write_outcomes(ledger, ranked, processed, Hosts(),
738	                   budget={'note': 're-derived from the ledger post hoc'})
739	    return 0
740	
741	
742	def main() -> int:
743	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
744	    sub = ap.add_subparsers(dest='cmd', required=True)
745	    sub.add_parser('build')
746	    pf = sub.add_parser('fetch')
747	    pf.add_argument('--max-units', type=int, default=600)
748	    pf.add_argument('--wall-clock-mins', type=float, default=45.0)
749	    pf.add_argument('--sample', type=int, default=0)
750	    pf.add_argument('--tiers', type=str, default='0,1,2')
751	    sub.add_parser('report')
752	    args = ap.parse_args()
753	    return {'build': cmd_build, 'fetch': cmd_fetch, 'report': cmd_report}[args.cmd](args)
754	
755	
756	if __name__ == '__main__':
757	    raise SystemExit(main())

The introduction of algorithms into a large number of industries has already restructured the landscape of
work and threatens to continue. While a growing body of CSCW research centered on the future of work
has begun to document these shifts, relatively little is known about workers’ experiences beyond those of
platform-mediated gig workers. In this paper, we turn to a traditional work sector, Amazon fulfillment centers
(FC), to deepen our field’s empirical examination of algorithmic management. Drawing on two years of
ethnographic research, we show how FC workers react to managers’ interventions, imposed productivity rates,
and quantified objectification when subjected to labor-tracking systems in their physical work environments.
Situating FC workers’ resistance to algorithmic systems and metrics within the current CSCW literature

content.content:
380	_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
381	_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
382	    r'nber working paper (?:series|no)|national bureau of economic research|'
383	    r'iza (?:dp|discussion paper)|this (?:draft|version):|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|'
384	    r'do not (?:cite|quote) without|arxiv:\d', re.I)
385	
386	
387	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
388	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
389	
390	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
391	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
392	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
393	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
394	    """
395	    words = text.split()
396	    header = text[:header_chars]
397	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
398	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
399	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
400	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
401	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
402	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
403	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
404	    return {
405	        'word_count': len(words),
406	        'char_count': len(text),
407	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
408	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
409	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
410	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(header)),
411	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(header)),
412	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
413	        'header_real_words': header_real,
414	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
415	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
416	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
417	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
418	        'identity_window': header,
419	        'header_text': header[:400],

640	    C_FULLTEXT:   'FULLTEXT',
641	    C_ABSTRACT:   'ABSTRACT_ONLY',
642	    C_CITATION:   'CITATION_ONLY',
643	    C_NOT_DOC:    'CITATION_ONLY',      # a cookie banner is NOT an abstract of the paper
644	    C_UNREADABLE: 'CITATION_ONLY',      # a PDF whose font never decoded is not a summary of anything
645	}
646	
647	#: The content class each artifact kind reports as. The finding-bearing kinds are absent on purpose:
648	#: for those, THE REGISTRY decides (complete => FULLTEXT), and hardcoding them here would be a second
649	#: opinion about completeness, which is the whole defect.
650	_CLASS_OF_KIND = {
651	    'extraction_failure': C_UNREADABLE,
652	    'landing_page':       C_NOT_DOC,
653	    'wrong_work':         C_NOT_DOC,
654	    'abstract':           C_ABSTRACT,
655	    'citation_only':      C_CITATION,
656	    'unknown':            C_CITATION,
657	}
658	
659	
660	def derive_artifact_kind(prof: dict, source_type: str = '') -> tuple[str, str]:
661	    """WHAT KIND OF DOCUMENT IS THIS? Registry-driven, from OBSERVATIONS only.
662	
663	    `source_type` is the kind of RECORD the unit came from — a Crossref `journal-article`, a court's
664	    opinions endpoint, ClinicalTrials.gov. It selects WHICH COMPLETENESS PROFILE APPLIES and nothing
665	    else, and it is REBUTTABLE BY THE BYTES: the demotions below run first, so a login wall served by
666	    a court's website is a login wall.
667	
668	    It is NOT `fulltext_source` wearing a new hat. `fulltext_source='working_paper'` was a claim about
669	    WHAT THE DOCUMENT IS, written by whichever script ran, believed by everyone, and false in both
670	    directions. `source_type` cannot promote anything: it cannot make a cookie banner an article, and
671	    it cannot make a working paper a journal version — the header stamp decides that, in
672	    derive_semantic_binding(), untouched. All it can do is stop us judging a judicial opinion by a
673	    journal article's word count.
674	    """
675	    wc = prof.get('word_count', 0)
676	    readable = prof.get('readable_word_count', 0)
677	    chrome = prof.get('chrome_markers', 0)
678	    glyph = prof.get('glyph_garbage_ratio', 0)
679	
680	    # Which profile do we judge this against? Default: the scholarly one (every row in this corpus is
681	    # a Crossref journal-article). An UNREGISTERED type is not silently accepted — it falls back to
682	    # scholarly and SAYS SO, because a kind nobody registered has no completeness profile to use.
683	    work_kind, _claimed = _SOURCE_TYPE.get((source_type or '').strip().lower(), ('study', ''))
684	    declared = _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind) or 'journal_article'
685	    spec = KIND_PROFILE[declared]
686	    floor = spec['stub_floor']
687	    src = f'source_type={source_type!r}' if source_type else 'no source_type declared (scholarly default)'
688	
689	    if wc <= 0:
690	        return 'citation_only', 'we hold no bytes at all'
691	    if glyph > GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX:
692	        return 'extraction_failure', (f'{glyph:.0%} of tokens are (cid:NN) glyph codes — the font '
693	                                      f'never decoded')
694	    # A cookie banner is not a short paper. It is NOT A PAPER — and that is true of a 300-word statute
695	    # section's landing page too, which is why the LENGTH half of this test applies only where there
696	    # IS a length below which a document is a fragment. Where there is not (stub_floor=None), only
697	    # web-furniture DENSITY may condemn it.
698	    chrome_per_kw = 1000.0 * chrome / max(1, wc)
699	    if chrome and (chrome_per_kw >= CHROME_PER_1K_MAX or (floor is not None and wc < floor)):
700	        return 'landing_page', (f'{chrome} site-chrome marker(s) in {wc}w ({chrome_per_kw:.0f}/1k) — '
701	                                f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
702	    if floor is not None and readable < floor:
703	        # A SCHOLARLY FRAGMENT. Which fragment it is, is the registry's abstract_floor — not a second
704	        # universal number invented here.
705	        af = spec['abstract_floor']
706	        if af is not None and readable < af:
707	            return 'citation_only', f'{readable:,} readable words — a citation stub, not even an abstract'
708	        return 'abstract', (f'{readable:,} readable words is below the stub floor for `{declared}` '
709	                            f'({floor:,}) — a fragment of the article, not the article')
710	    return declared, f'{readable:,} readable words; judged as `{declared}` ({src})'
711	
712	
713	def _source_type_of(events: list[Event]) -> str:
714	    """The record type OBSERVED for this unit. An observation about what we asked for, with a basis."""
715	    for e in reversed(_observations(events)):
716	        st = e.payload.get('source_type')
717	        if st:
718	            return str(st)
719	    return ''
720	
721	
722	#: WHICH DOCUMENT WE HOLD IS BETTER. Used ONLY to pick which of several manifestations a unit-level
723	#: question is about — never to decide what any one of them IS.
724	_CLASS_RANK = {C_FULLTEXT: 4, C_ABSTRACT: 3, C_CITATION: 2, C_NOT_DOC: 1, C_UNREADABLE: 1}
725	
726	
727	def _holdings(events: list[Event]) -> list[tuple[dict, dict]]:
728	    """Every (manifestation, ITS OWN profile) PAIR this unit holds, in order.
729	
730	    `record_manifestation()` emits MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED adjacently, so a
731	    pair is a document and the profile OF THAT DOCUMENT. Pairing matters: without it, a unit holding a
732	    body and an abstract would answer identity questions from one and completeness questions from the
733	    other, and the answer would describe no document that exists.
734	    """
735	    pairs: list[tuple[dict, dict]] = []
736	    pending: dict | None = None
737	    for e in _observations(events):
738	        if e.kind == EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED:
739	            pending = e.payload
740	        elif e.kind == EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED:
741	            pairs.append((pending or {}, e.payload))
742	            pending = None
743	    return pairs
744	
745	
746	def _classify(prof: dict, source_type: str) -> tuple[str, dict]:
747	    """ONE document's bytes -> its content class. The whole of the completeness rule, for one blob."""
748	    kind, basis = derive_artifact_kind(prof, source_type)
749	    readable = prof.get('readable_word_count', 0) or prof.get('word_count', 0)
750	    verdict = 'CORRUPT' if prof.get('glyph_garbage_ratio', 0) > GLYPH_GARBAGE_MAX else 'CLEAN'
751	    # THE ONE REDUCER. Not a number in this file.
752	    complete, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, readable, verdict)
753	    cls = _CLASS_OF_KIND.get(kind)
754	    if cls is None:                       # a finding-bearing kind: THE REGISTRY says if it is whole
755	        cls = C_FULLTEXT if complete else C_ABSTRACT
756	    info = {'reason': basis if complete else '; '.join(reasons) or basis,
757	            'artifact_kind': kind, 'complete': complete, **prof}
758	    return cls, info
759	
760	
761	def _best_holding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[dict, dict, str, dict] | None:
762	    """THE BEST DOCUMENT WE HOLD for this unit -> (manifestation, profile, class, info).
763	
764	    IT USED TO TAKE THE LAST ONE PROFILED, AND THAT WAS A LIVE DEFECT — introduced, of course, in the
765	    fix for the defect it is. Once acquisition began retaining EVERY set of bytes instead of deleting
766	    all but one, a work commonly held two: the fetched body AND the bibliography's abstract. The
767	    abstract was ingested second, so `for e in events: prof = e.payload` ended holding the ABSTRACT'S
768	    profile — and the unit's label was derived from it.
769	
770	    Result, on the real corpus, before this was fixed:
771	
772	        Damioli (2021)   label = CITATION_ONLY    while the row carried 13,085 words
773	        Tolan (2021)     label = ABSTRACT         while the row carried 18,225 words
774	
775	    A label that asserts less than its content supports is the same disease as one that asserts more:
776	    it is a claim about a document nobody is holding. Five rows had it, and every one of them would
777	    have been skipped by the miner.
778	
779	    So the question "what do we hold for this work?" is answered by THE BEST DOCUMENT WE HOLD. A cookie
780	    banner sitting beside a journal article does not make the article an abstract; we hold the article.
781	    """
782	    pairs = _holdings(events)
783	    if not pairs:
784	        return None
785	    st = _source_type_of(events)
786	    scored = []
787	    for manif, prof in pairs:
788	        cls, info = _classify(prof, st)
789	        scored.append((_CLASS_RANK.get(cls, 0), prof.get('readable_word_count', 0) or 0,
790	                       manif, prof, cls, info))
791	    best = max(scored, key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1]))

797	    and it is never a number this module keeps to itself."""
798	    best = _best_holding(events)

800	        return C_CITATION, {'reason': 'no content was ever profiled'}
801	    _manif, _prof, cls, info = best
802	    n = len(_holdings(events))
803	    if n > 1:
804	        info = {**info, 'n_documents_held': n,
805	                'reason': f'{info["reason"]} (the best of {n} documents held for this work)'}
806	    return cls, info

I have the complete, byte-verified picture. Every reportable source is bound to `byte_sha256` + `text_sha256`, and the http tally confirms **200×132 / 403×24 / zero 429s** — the barrier is pure access-control, not throttling. Here is the census.

---

# THE HONEST ADMISSION CENSUS — outputs/acquisition_campaign/

## 0. THE FINDING THAT DOMINATES ALL OTHERS: the run did not run

The fetch worker was **killed ~63 seconds into a 55-minute / 900-unit budget** — no traceback, no `budget_stopped`, no `write_outcomes` (the `fetch_outcomes.json` on disk is stale, from a prior 8-unit/5-min *sample*). Across **both** run segments the ledger holds **16 distinct units, ever — out of a 5,061-unit union (0.3%).** 2,571 units carried an *offline* OA URL that cost zero network and were never spent.

So this is a census of a **barely-started run, not of the access ceiling.** Within the 16 attempted, the ceiling was access; across the corpus, the ceiling is *runtime* — the process needs to actually complete. Both are real, and they are different findings.

All 16 attempted were **Tier-0 empty-cell** targets (the `m2_empty_cells` scout), so what we see is exactly the hardest cells — which is informative.

## 1. Provenance graph over the 16 — artifact_kind/completeness derived FROM THE BYTES

I re-ran the on-disk reducers (`event_ledger.derive_content_profile / _semantic_binding / _route_status`) read-only over the ledger (`outputs/acquisition_campaign/census_probe.py`), then hand-checked every "FULLTEXT" against its actual extracted-text blob. **The reducer over-admits by 3** — two version-mismatch manifestations and one predatory landing page it scores as journal full text:

| unit | reducer says | THE BYTES say | my verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10.1016/j.caeai.2025.100476 | FULLTEXT / SAME_WORK / 17,247w | locator = `econstor.eu/.../GLO-DP-1668.pdf`; header = "**Working Paper … GLO Discussion Paper No. 1668 … Global Labor Organization**" | **DISCOVERY LEAD** (working paper, not the Elsevier C&E:AI article) |
| 10.1145/3757409 | FULLTEXT / SAME_WORK / 16,172w | locator = `arxiv.org/pdf/2508.09438`; arXiv watermark in header | **DISCOVERY LEAD** (arXiv preprint, not the ACM/PACMHCI version) |
| 10.70211/bafr.v1i1.178 | FULLTEXT / 1,919w | title+abstract+chrome; tail = "Published by **WISE Pendidikan Indonesia**", predatory Vol 1/Iss 1 | **QUARANTINE** (predatory landing/thin) |

The `caeai` miss is structural, not a fluke: `_PREPRINT_STAMP` (event_ledger.py:381) knows `nber|iza|arxiv:\d|this version:` but **not** "GLO Discussion Paper" or a bare "Working Paper" heading, so `preprint_stamp_in_header=false` and binding defaults to SAME_WORK. The stale `fetch_outcomes.json` already counts this GLO working paper as an "education FETCHED" — i.e. the pipeline *would ship a working paper as a journal full text* today. That is the exact failure THE LAW names ("Acemoglu's robot effect was 0.37pp in the WP and 0.2pp in the JPE").

## 2. Admissibility under the JOURNAL-ONLY policy (all 16)

**ADMISSIBLE — bound, complete, journal-attributable, on-topic (5):**
1. **BMC Nursing** 2024 — Sommer et al., *Nurses' perceptions/knowledge of AI (Germany)* — 6,059w — healthcare
2. **Nursing Reports** (MDPI) 2024 — Alsaedi et al., *Perceived worries adopting AI among healthcare professionals (Saudi Arabia)* — 6,345w — healthcare
3. **Open Forum Infectious Diseases** (OUP) 2024 — Langford & Branch-Elliman et al., *Confronting disruption of the ID workforce by AI* — 5,486w — healthcare
4. **Bulletin of the WHO** 2025 — Occhipinti et al., *AI, recessionary pressures & population health* — 5,892w — healthcare/health-policy
5. **PNAS Nexus** (OUP) 2024 — Septiandri, Constantinides, Quercia, *Potential impact of AI innovations on US occupations* — 8,143w — general/cross-occupation (frontier)

**ADMISSIBLE-BUT-LOW-QUALITY — mechanically passes, predatory-tier, flagged (1):**
6. **IJFMR** 2024 — Jadhav & Banubakode, *Implications of AI on the Employment Sector* — 3,549w — student-authored essay in a pay-to-publish venue. On-topic + complete + has DOI/ISSN, but not credible evidence. **Recommend exclude.**

**DISCOVERY LEAD — preprint/working-paper bytes (2):** caeai→GLO-DP-1668; 10.1145/3757409→arXiv:2508.09438.

**QUARANTINE — abstract-only / landing (4):** 10.1111/ntwe.12272 (Cadmus abstract, 293w), 10.36922/aih.4903 (journal landing, 1,089w), 10.1111/ntwe.12282 (EconStor landing, 269w), 10.70211/bafr.v1i1.178 (predatory landing, 1,919w).

**NO CONTENT — transport failure, NOT absence, retriable via another route (4):** 10.1016/j.iref.2025.104292 (Elsevier JS "Redirecting"), 10.1016/j.joitmc.2025.100607 (Elsevier 403+redirect), 10.3390/buildings15060933 (MDPI 403), 10.70445/gjcsai.1.1.2025.43-54 (predatory; process died mid-fetch — this is the last ledger event).

## 3. THE CENSUS

**NEW admissible journal works: 10 → 15** (the five above). **→ 16 only if the predatory IJFMR is admitted.** From 16 attempted units — this is a floor set by a killed run, not a ceiling.

**Empty cells now ≥2 independent admissible works:**
- **healthcare: 4 ✓** — BMC Nursing, Nursing Reports, OFID, Bull WHO. **The only cell that clears the bar.**
- manufacturing / retail / finance / education / agriculture / transport: **0 credible each.** Best case is one *dubious* item per cell (a preprint, a predatory venue, or an abstract). The m2 cell tags are loose keyword co-occurrence — Bull WHO was tagged "finance," PNAS Nexus "healthcare+transport," IJFMR four cells at once; by the bytes none of those hold.
- Structural note: the reason healthcare closed and the others did not is **route, not topic** — PMC/EuropePMC exposes health-sciences full text openly; Wiley/Elsevier/MDPI-direct/ACM/OUP-direct returned 403 or JS walls. And the industrial cells are "empty" partly because credible journals under-cover AI-and-work there, so the keyword net into them disproportionately surfaces predatory venues (BAFR, IJFMR, GJCSAI, AccScience-AIH all appeared here).

**2023-2025 frontier admitted: 5 of 5** (BMC Nursing '24, Nursing Reports '24, OFID '24, Bull WHO '25, PNAS Nexus '24; +IJFMR '24 if counted). The new material is uniformly current — the pre-2023 recency gap is addressed *in kind*, just not *in volume*.

**Genuine CONTRASTS gained (disagree with the seed): ~0.** None of the five numerically contradict Acemoglu/Goos/Damioli/etc. The four healthcare studies are perception/survey evidence in a **new sector** — a thematic counter-note (worker apprehension vs. the seed's productivity-optimism) but not a numeric contrast. PNAS Nexus (2024) is a task-level occupational-exposure study that **extends** Tolan 2021 rather than disputing it. Value added = coverage + recency, not disagreement.

**Honestly unreachable (within the 16):**
- **throttled-retriable (429): 0.** Zero 429/503 anywhere in the ledger. The 24 blocked events are all **403** (Wiley 6, MDPI 5, DOAJ 4, doi.org 3, uni-goettingen 2, OUP 2, ScienceDirect 2) plus Elsevier JS-redirect walls. Nothing here is "wait and retry"; it is publisher access-control.
- **paywalled / no-journal-OA: ~7** — iref, joitmc, caeai (journal version; only the GLO WP was OA), 3757409 (only arXiv was OA), ntwe.12272, ntwe.12282, aih.4903, buildings. The journal manifestation was blocked and the only OA copy was a preprint / working paper / abstract / landing.

## 4. WHAT A MERGE WOULD ADD (not merged — A6's scored decision). Every binding:

| # | work | cell | route/adapter | content_type | byte_sha256 | text_sha256 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | BMC Nursing 2024 | healthcare | bmcnurs.biomedcentral.com (OA PDF) | pdf | `d22b6a1cabb4d30c757a246a` | `2a7c717d21e924fef6f76e52` |
| 2 | Nursing Reports 2024 | healthcare | EuropePMC XML (PMC11677610) | xml | `547cd055cb0f9a6d3b5fad69` | `2396a895bbbffd37732ad7c8` |
| 3 | Open Forum Infect Dis 2024 | healthcare | NCBI PMC HTML (PMC10906702) | html | `b4091ca0d7c8551e7f73bc48` | `3ef64742e245556cabd5023c` |
| 4 | Bulletin of the WHO 2025 | healthcare/policy | EuropePMC XML (PMC11774225) | xml | `ed1ba061f2a6423695def790` | `1ccacf1835f76eb4b6807ebe` |
| 5 | PNAS Nexus 2024 | general/occupations | EuropePMC XML (PMC11421150) | xml | `1c14814a494a2a1c373f372b` | `4a9410cf2582aead2c7ca0b2` |
| (6) | IJFMR 2024 *(predatory — recommend exclude)* | employment | ijfmr.com (PDF) | pdf | `5f88138a6bc1728d6cc00b22` | `cb7923b570ca676a0df97524` |

A merge would add **+5 credible works (all 2024-25, all content-hash-bound), closing only the healthcare cell**; it would add **0 genuine contrasts** and close **0 of** manufacturing/retail/finance/education/agriculture/transport. Do NOT let the pipeline's own accounting slip in the two discovery leads (caeai/GLO byte `bec169024cb8ba1096967689`; arXiv byte `7f7b62f5e26f6ff8c372e8ec`) — the reducer scores both as SAME_WORK journal full text and they are not.

## Bottom line
Max-effort search did not yield 25, or 60 — it yielded **5** credible new journal works, because **the run was killed at 0.3% of the union**. That is a **runtime/orchestration failure, not an access ceiling.** But the 16 units it did touch expose a sharp, actionable access pattern: **PMC/EuropePMC = 5/5 clean journal full text; every publisher-direct economics/management route (Elsevier, Wiley, MDPI-direct, ACM, OUP-direct) = 403 or JS wall or preprint-only OA.** Re-running to completion is the single highest-value next step (2,571 offline-OA hints are sitting unused and the fetcher is idempotent/resumable); and to reach the non-health cells at all, the corpus will need either the PMC-indexed subset mined deeply or a paid/authenticated route — the free OA copies of those journals are overwhelmingly preprints and abstracts.

Reproducible probe: `/home/polaris/polaris_project/outputs/acquisition_campaign/census_probe.py`. Ledger: `/home/polaris/polaris_project/outputs/acquisition_campaign/fetch_ledger.jsonl` (450 events, 16 units). Blobs: `/home/polaris/polaris_project/outputs/acquisition_campaign/blobs/`.
